- Word order issues

Given the grammar errors and vocabulary range, this is likely A2 level.

- 10 sentences
- Tips on how to organize days to have more free time

## 3) Error Markup

Looking at the errors identified by the expert annotation, I need to categorize them by type and severity to determine the appropriate CEFR level.

Spelling errors include "Hellow" (should be "Hello"), "wery" (should be "very"), "enouth" (should be "enough"), and "bust" (should be "boost"). Grammar issues involve "me to" (should be "me too"), "In first semester" (needs article "the"), "wery big problem" (adverb-adjective mismatch), "how can I fix" (word order problem), "I will give you them" (should be "I will give them to you"), "some task-management system" (needs article "a"), and "in uni" (should be "at uni"). Word order problems include "how can I fix" and "I will give you them". The text organization is good with numbered list, and "prioritize" and "do sport" are appropriate lexical choices.

## 4) Strengths

The text demonstrates solid organizational structure through its numbered list format, uses relevant vocabulary like "prioritize" and "task-management system," and maintains an appropriate semi-formal tone for the context. The writer successfully conveys advice despite grammatical and spelling errors.

## 5) Grammar Score
